subject: UX & Research
title: The Stanford Prison Experiment's UX Lesson
description: A 60-second lesson on The Stanford Prison Experiment's UX Lesson.

hook.text: Zimbardo's 1971 prison study showed environments shape behavior. Guards grew cruel, prisoners passive.

buildup.text: The environment's design — uniforms, cells, rules — transformed ordinary students into their assigned roles.

discovery.text: In digital products, the interface is the environment. Dark patterns and urgency timers shape user behavior.

twist.text: The experiment was stopped after 6 days. The environment changed behavior so fast it became dangerous.

climax.text: Designers create environments that shape millions of behaviors daily. That's power that demands responsibility.

punchline.text: Interfaces shape behavior like environments shape people. Design responsibly.

quiz.question: What UX principle does the Stanford Prison Experiment illustrate?
options: Environments (including digital interfaces) powerfully shape human behavior
